reviews.review_title: Awesome concept, needs to be more/harder
reviews.review_description:
I think I'm a perfect person to review this game.  I call Oh Shape one of my favorite games so this was already right up my alley.  And I also practice some martial arts, so this was really a no brainer for me to try.  And no surprise, I liked it!  There's a lot of potential here.

That said, here are some minor concerns, and why I withhold one star to leave them room to improve (besides that the devs did not respond to my email  :) ):
1) There's no height calibration, and even at 6' I can't reach most of the high arcs.
2) I liked the idea of multiple martial arts, but I didn't realize they would be only one level each.  I thought there would be several levels per martial art.  Needs more variety than just 10 levels, ideally at least 1,2,3-star per martial art.  Some mechanics that must have been hard to program in, like the swords, are underused.
3) It's a little slow.  I use my Quest for cardio, and even 3* didn't move my HR enough.  It's also hard to connect to the full forms the instructors perform.  Why not kill 2 birds with one stone and put more of the intermediate postures in as well?  That will make us move faster and better.  Drum beats could be faster also, and maybe more left and right to give us leg movements.  (Shouldn't the big drum be purple??  Minor gripe lol.)

Awesome in concept, just hope it tidies up a bit and expands a lot.  As a programmer, I think these are all "easy" enough to fix.  Getting the mechanics to work is hard.  Adding more content is (relatively) easy.  Good luck!

developer_response.body:
Dear PJ 

Thanks a ton for taking the time to leave such a detailed review! Your support means a lot to us, and we’re really happy to hear that you enjoy the core gameplay of our game! We’ve taken note of all your suggestions, and they’ll definitely help us make the game even better.

As for the points you mentioned, we’re already brainstorming how to improve! While the specifics are still being discussed, your ideas have given us a lot of inspiration. Things like height calibration, adding more levels and moves, and speeding up the pace are all on our radar, and we’ll do our best to work them into future updates.

Thanks again for your support and patience! If you have any other thoughts or questions, feel free to hit us up anytime. Happy gaming!

Cheers,
Gulita Studio Team

reviews.review_description:
Sound like a good app, maybe the postures are real ones from kung-fu, and I would like to learn them but there are still some adjustments to make:
. The floor is not at your feets location, making you feel taller, but making also the models bigger and your posture don't match always ;
. The postures is a bit ambiguous, you don't know if you need to mirror or match the posture, very confusing.
. The swords are impossible to set in the indicated positions because you cannot turn your wrist by 90°.
. The drums hammers are in a position where it is not easy to hit the drums. The double drums should be both colors.
This game has te potential to become a good one, but please, it nees some polishing...

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your detailed feedback and support for our game! Here’s how we’ve addressed the issues you mentioned:
	1.	Floor Position Issue: This issue has been resolved in the latest version. The floor calibration is now more precise, ensuring it better matches the player’s height and position for an improved immersive experience.
	2.	Posture Understanding Issue: We’ve optimized the scale and proportions of the POSE system, making the actions more realistic and aligned, which results in a smoother and more natural experience.

As for the weapon control and drumstick positioning suggestions, we are actively exploring improvements and will continue refining these areas to ensure the controls feel more intuitive and enjoyable. Your patience and feedback are invaluable to us! 😊

If you have more suggestions or notice anything new, feel free to share. We’re excited for you to experience the latest version! 🎮✨

reviews.review_description: It's like that other shape fitting game but better because the moves are real.  Pose detection works well.  It's a pretty good workout even though it looks slow you have to move pretty fast and it stretches your body in different ways than most games.  it has some problems. Feels like the play space is tilted and it feels like you're standing 2 feet off the ground.   There are 10 stages they are pretty long and replayable.  The question people are probably wondering is can you use this to learn real skills?  I would say maybe yes!

developer_response.body:
Hello !

This is the KungFu VR Team.Thank you for taking the time to write this review of our game! We are very glad to hear that you have enjoyed our game!

We are improving the issues you mentioned in next version. At the same time, we are producing more fun content. In the meantime, why not hit up the current KungFu courses to level up your skills and snag some more achievements?

And thank you for the awesome suggestion! We really appreciate it. Your fantastic ideas and praise provide a lot of help for our team to continue to improve.

Again, we thank you for your time for writing this great review and we hope you have a great day ahead of you!
Sincerely,

The KungFu VR  Team
